title: Docker compatibility mode insufficient for grafana
body:
### Issue Description

One of the steps of configuring grafana for local development is:

```
make devenv sources=influxdb,loki
```

With podman, and docker compatibility turned on, I'm getting:

> unable to get image 'influxdb:latest': Cannot connect to the Docker daemon at unix:///Users/jasonmarshall/.docker/run/docker.sock. Is the docker daemon running?

`docker pull influxdb:latest` of course succeeds. So it's some disagreement between the grafana build and podman.



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. On Mac
2. follow the grafana developer-guide.md
3. get to `make devenv sources=influxdb,loki`
4. pout


### Describe the results you received

Deleting docker-compose.yaml
Adding Compose header to docker-compose.yaml
Adding docker/blocks/influxdb/docker-compose.yaml to docker-compose.yaml
Adding docker/blocks/loki/docker-compose.yaml to docker-compose.yaml
unable to get image 'influxdb:latest': Cannot connect to the Docker daemon at unix:///Users/jasonmarshall/.docker/run/docker.sock. Is the docker daemon running?
make: *** [devenv] Error 1


### Describe the results you expected

Clean run

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Sonoma, M2 macbook pro

### Additional information

Manually pulling the images it complains about doesn't suffice because it eventually tries to look for a local image and cannot find it.

This happens both with podman 1.17 and 1.18

comments.body:
Umm...  

Update: I came back a couple of hours later to my computer telling me that podman had crashed (which is deeply concerning in and of itself), and would I like to restart it?

And now this problem is no longer happening. So I don't know what wedged state it got itself into. Apple has a crash dump but I don't think that helps you.

> make devenv sources=influxdb,loki

Note that is not a useful reproducer for us, we have no time to figure out what that does of if the issue would even be with podman, etc... We would like to get a reproducer with actual podman commands or API queries.

If you have a stack trace then sure that can be helpful to share as it sounds like something crashed.
And note your podman version inside the machine is quite old. It would be best to recreate or update the machine and then try again. And you could try to reproduce with the latest version such as 5.5.0.-rc2 https://github.com/containers/podman/releases/tag/v5.5.0-rc2, the final 5.5.0 version will be released later today so you could also wait for that to test

A reviewer has determined we need more information to understand the reported issue.  A comment on what is missing should be provided. Be certain you:

* provide an exact reproducer where possible
* verify you have provided all relevant information - minimum is `podman info`
* answer any follow up questions

If no response to the `needs-info` is provided in 30 days, this issue may be closed by our stale bot.

For more information on reporting issues on this repository, consult our [issue guide](ISSUE.md).

This is largely a consequence of #24254

But the next error is 

https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/22881

Both of these are marked not planned.

Yes link support will never be added, it has been deprecated for many years. I suggest you move to using proper networks which are well supported. 

For the hosts error that is different context, if you are talking about macos this is #21681.

The implication that I can control hundreds of other people is flattering but misplaced. I, just an outsider trying to find a project I can use as a foil to freshen my front end skills. I’ll never be a core dev.
